header.topic: Automating Your PowerPoint and Final Improvements

learning_sequence_context.position_in_sequence: Lesson 9 of 10 in the Term 1-2 sequence - Lesson 7 of the 8-lesson PowerPoint unit.
learning_sequence_context.previous_lesson_summary: Last lesson pupils added an internal and an external URL to their 'About Me' presentation.
learning_sequence_context.next_lesson_link: Next lesson pupils present their completed 'About Me' PowerPoint to the class, concluding the unit.

expected_outcomes.lesson_objective: Pupils know that PowerPoints can be automated, understand how to apply the correct settings for a timed, self-running presentation, and can automate their 'About Me' presentation while making final improvements against the term's assessment criteria.
assessment_criteria: Each student will set appropriate slide timings using rehearse timings or manual entry.
Each student will produce a presentation that runs automatically without manual clicking.

Each student will check their presentation against the unit's assessment criteria (mind-map-based structure, manipulated images, theme/transitions/animations, sound/video, internal and external URLs) and make at least one final improvement.
expected_outcomes.activity_summary: Individual application of slide timing/automation settings, followed by a self-review against the whole unit's assessment criteria and final improvements ahead of presenting.

key_vocabulary.definition: A PowerPoint feature that records how long a presenter spends on each slide, to set automatic timings.
A presentation that advances through its slides automatically, without a presenter clicking through it.

preparation.additional_adult_role: Support pupils in setting realistic slide timings (neither too fast to read nor too slow to hold attention).
accessibility: Printed unit checklist available as an alternative to an on-screen version for the self-review

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Ask pupils to note, on mini-whiteboards, where their internal and external links go on their presentation.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Recall and note their internal and external link destinations.

check_for_understanding.question: What is the difference between an internal and an external URL?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Proceed if most pupils correctly distinguish the two; otherwise briefly recap.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Explain that a PowerPoint can be set to advance automatically, and introduce the rehearse-timings and manual-timing settings.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch and identify where the timing settings are found.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Model rehearsing timings on a sample presentation, then play it back in slideshow mode to show it advancing automatically.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch and note whether the automatic timing felt too fast, too slow, or about right.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Set the task of automating their presentation and then reviewing it against the unit assessment criteria checklist, making at least one final improvement; circulate with least-help-first prompts.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Set slide timings to automate their presentation, then review it against the checklist (mind-map-based structure, images, theme/transitions/animations, sound/video, internal and external URLs) and make final improvements.

check_for_understanding.question: Does your presentation run automatically from start to finish without you clicking?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils whose automation fails are supported individually before moving to the checklist review.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Facilitate a paired final checklist review; cold-call two pairs to share one improvement they made this lesson and why.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Swap with a partner, check the presentation against the full unit checklist, and confirm it is ready to present next lesson.

check_for_understanding.question: Exit ticket: which item on the unit checklist did you improve today?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils whose presentation is missing a checklist item are flagged to complete it before next lesson's presenting session.
